engineGuide.h2: Vauxhall Mokka Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide

entries.title: 1.7L Diesel
entries.history: Mainstream 1.7L CDTi workhorse for first-gen Mokka; strong torque but EGR cooler cracking common.
compatibleVariants: SE, Elite, Sri, Design, Tech Line, Exclusiv (1.7 CDTi trims)
commonFailures: EGR cooler cracking causing coolant loss (60k-90k miles)
Timing chain tensioner wear (80k+ miles)
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey use

Injector seal deterioration

commonProblems.h2: Vauxhall Mokka Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why A14NET Engines Rattle on Cold Start
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Mokka SE, Sri, Design, Elite, Exclusiv (2012-2019, A14NET 1.4L Turbo)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing chain slack that causes metallic rattling on cold starts and risks valve timing jump.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides and seals before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents valve damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt A14NET with upgraded tensioner, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value once rattling confirmed; avoids repeated repairs

problems.recommendation: If you hear a distinct rattling noise on cold start from your A14NET Mokka, act immediately. A preventative chain kit replacement at a specialist can save the engine, but once the chain has jumped timing, only a rebuilt unit offers reliable long-term value.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt A14NET engine prices for your Vauxhall Mokka

problems.h4: EGR Cooler Cracking - Why A17DTR Diesel Engines Lose Coolant
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Mokka SE, Elite, Tech Line (2012-2019, A17DTR 1.7L CDTi)
problems.rootCause: Thermal cycling causes hairline cracks in the EGR cooler core, allowing exhaust gases to contaminate coolant and trigger overheating warnings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cracked EGR cooler, flush cooling system, reset fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if engine hasn't overheated; monitor closely

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt A17DTR with upgraded EGR components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if coolant contamination caused bearing wear

problems.recommendation: Coolant loss with white smoke or sweet exhaust smell on your A17DTR Mokka signals EGR cooler failure. If caught before overheating, cooler replacement at a specialist is cost-effective. If the engine has run hot, a rebuilt unit prevents repeat failures.
problems.cta: Get quotes for A17DTR Vauxhall Mokka diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Timing Belt Premature Wear - B12XFL PureTech Engine Concern
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Mokka GS Line, Ultimate, Elite (2020-2024, B12XFL 1.2L PureTech)
problems.rootCause: PSA-sourced timing belt material degrades faster than scheduled intervals, risking belt snap and catastrophic interference engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, water pump and seals ahead of failure
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance; prevents catastrophic engine damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt B12XFL with updated belt specification, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if belt has snapped and caused valve/piston damage

problems.recommendation: The B12XFL PureTech requires proactive belt replacement regardless of mileage. If your Mokka has over 40k miles and the belt hasn't been changed, schedule a specialist inspection immediately - prevention is vastly cheaper than replacement.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt B12XFL engine prices for your Vauxhall Mokka

problems.h4: DPF Regeneration Failure - B16DTH Diesel Warning Light
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Mokka SE, Elite, Tech Line (2015-2019, B16DTH 1.6L CDTi)
problems.rootCause: Short-journey urban driving prevents complete DPF regeneration cycles, causing soot buildup that triggers warning lights and eventual engine limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Professional DPF cleaning or replacement, ECU reset, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine hasn't entered prolonged limp mode

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt B16DTH with DPF system check, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if soot contamination caused turbo or bearing damage

problems.recommendation: DPF warning lights on your B16DTH Mokka often respond to specialist cleaning if addressed early. However, prolonged limp mode or repeated regeneration failures suggest deeper contamination - a rebuilt engine offers better long-term reliability for high-mileage urban drivers.
problems.cta: Get quotes for B16DTH Vauxhall Mokka diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: AdBlue System Fault - B15DT BlueHDi No-Start Condition
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Mokka GS Line, Ultimate (2020-2024, B15DT 1.5L BlueHDi)
problems.rootCause: AdBlue injector clogging or NOx sensor failure triggers ECU lockout preventing engine start, a common PSA-platform issue.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace AdBlue injector/NOx sensor, flush tank, reset ECU adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if fault is isolated to sensor/injector components

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt B15DT with AdBlue system validation, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Consider if repeated AdBlue faults indicate broader emissions system degradation

problems.recommendation: AdBlue faults on B15DT Mokka often resolve with sensor or injector replacement at a specialist. However, if your vehicle has multiple emissions-related warnings, a rebuilt engine with validated AdBlue components provides greater peace of mind for second-gen owners.
problems.cta: Enquire about B15DT Vauxhall Mokka BlueHDi engine availability

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Vauxhall Mokka
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with A14NET timing chain rattle, A17DTR EGR cooler leaks, or B12XFL timing belt concerns, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Vauxhall Mokka specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Vauxhall Mokka Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Vauxhall Mokka and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

fuelTypes.h2: Vauxhall Mokka Engines by Fuel Type

items.title: Vauxhall Mokka Diesel Engines
items.description: First-gen Mokka relied on 1.7L/1.6L CDTi diesels, while second-gen adopted PSA-sourced 1.5L BlueHDi units - all offering strong torque but requiring attention to EGR, DPF and AdBlue systems.
items.descriptor: First-gen Mokka relied on 1.7L/1.6L CDTi diesels, while second-gen adopted PSA-sourced 1.5L BlueHDi units - all offering strong torque but requiring attention to EGR, DPF and AdBlue systems.
items.familiesLabel: Common Vauxhall Mokka Diesel Engine Families
families: A17DTR - mainstream 1.7L CDTi (SE/Elite), EGR cooler cracking common
B16DTH - efficient 1.6L CDTi (Tech Line), DPF regeneration sensitivity
B15DT - PSA 1.5L BlueHDi (2020+), AdBlue system fault prone
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and towing capability
EGR cooler cracking on A17 series (60k-100k miles)
DPF regeneration issues on urban short-journey use
AdBlue system faults causing no-start on B15DT
items.cta: Get quotes for Vauxhall Mokka Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Vauxhall Mokka Petrol Engines
items.description: First-gen Mokka featured 1.4L/1.6L turbo and NA petrol engines, while second-gen adopted PSA PureTech units - all requiring attention to timing components and carbon management.
items.descriptor: First-gen Mokka featured 1.4L/1.6L turbo and NA petrol engines, while second-gen adopted PSA PureTech units - all requiring attention to timing components and carbon management.
items.familiesLabel: Common Vauxhall Mokka Petrol Engine Families
families: A14NET - high-volume 1.4L turbo, timing chain tensioner wear dominant failure
A16LET - performance 1.6L turbo, wastegate actuator and carbon issues
B12XFL - PSA 1.2L PureTech, timing belt premature wear concern
knownFor: Lively turbo performance and lower upfront cost versus diesel
Timing chain tensioner rattle on cold start (A14NET)
Timing belt degradation requiring proactive replacement (B12XFL)
Direct injection carbon buildup affecting performance

engineYears.h2: Vauxhall Mokka Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Vauxhall Mokka's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: Launch years introduced the A14NET petrol and A17DTR diesel that would dominate Mokka replacement demand for a decade.
keyChanges: Vauxhall Mokka launched in UK with GM Gamma II platform
A14NET 1.4L turbo petrol and A17DTJ/A17DTR 1.7L CDTi engines introduced
Front-wheel drive standard, iWD optional on select trims
mainEngines: A17DTR - 1.7L diesel main code (SE, Elite, Exclusiv)
knownFor: Strong initial sales with competitive SUV positioning
A14NET timing chain tensioner issues emerging in owner forums
A17DTR EGR cooler cracking reports from higher-mileage examples
Solid build quality but interior materials criticised versus rivals
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2014 Vauxhall Mokka replacement engines
years.ticker: Vauxhall Mokka A14NET timing chain rattle • Vauxhall Mokka A17DTR coolant leak • Vauxhall Mokka 1.4 turbo engine replacement
enquiries: Vauxhall Mokka A14NET timing chain rattle

years.description: Mid-cycle update introduced Euro 6-compliant B16DTH diesel and updated A17FEL code, addressing emissions while retaining core architecture.
keyChanges: Facelift styling updates and interior trim revisions
B16DTH 1.6L CDTi introduced replacing A17DTR on select trims
Euro 6 emissions compliance with AdBlue readiness on newer diesels
mainEngines: B16DTH - 1.6L diesel Euro 6 compliant (Tech Line, Elite)
knownFor: Improved refinement and equipment levels post-facelift
B16DTH DPF regeneration sensitivity on urban driving cycles
A14NET timing chain concerns now widely documented in owner communities
Strong residual values supporting replacement engine demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2016 Vauxhall Mokka replacement engines
years.ticker: Vauxhall Mokka B16DTH DPF warning • Vauxhall Mokka A17FEL EGR repair • Vauxhall Mokka 1.6 diesel engine replacement

years.description: Final production years of first-gen Mokka saw stable engine line-up with A14NET and B16DTH dominating replacement enquiries as vehicles entered higher-mileage territory.
keyChanges: Special edition trims (Red Edition, Black Edition) with cosmetic updates
Engine line-up stabilised with A14NET petrol and B16DTH diesel as core units
iWD availability reduced as focus shifted to second-generation development
mainEngines: B16DTH - 1.6L diesel Euro 6 (SE, Elite, Tech Line)
A16XER - 1.6L NA petrol (entry trims, declining availability)
knownFor: High-mileage examples now requiring major engine work
A14NET timing chain replacement becoming routine maintenance item
B16DTH AdBlue system compatibility queries on retrofit scenarios
Strong aftermarket support for replacement engines and parts
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2019 Vauxhall Mokka replacement engines
years.ticker: Vauxhall Mokka A14NET rebuilt engine • Vauxhall Mokka B16DTH AdBlue fault • Vauxhall Mokka 2018 engine replacement

years.description: All-new second-generation Mokka adopted PSA CMP platform with PureTech petrol and BlueHDi diesel engines, introducing new failure patterns and replacement considerations.
keyChanges: Complete platform change to PSA CMP/e-CMP architecture
B12XFL 1.2L PureTech petrol and B15DT 1.5L BlueHDi diesel introduced
Electric Mokka-e variant launched alongside combustion engines
Mild hybrid (MHEV) options added to select trims
mainEngines: B12XFL - 1.2L PureTech turbo petrol (GS Line, Ultimate)
knownFor: Modern styling and technology but higher replacement engine costs
B12XFL timing belt concerns requiring proactive replacement scheduling
B15DT AdBlue system faults causing no-start conditions
Limited specialist rebuild availability versus first-gen engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020-2024 Vauxhall Mokka replacement engines
years.ticker: Vauxhall Mokka B12XFL timing belt • Vauxhall Mokka B15DT AdBlue fault • Vauxhall Mokka 2021 engine replacement

faq.h2: Vauxhall Mokka Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Vauxhall Mokka engine replacement cost?
items.answer: Vauxhall Mokka engine replacement costs typically range from £1,195 to £3,190 for rebuilt units depending on engine code and variant, with petrol A14NET at the lower end and second-gen B12XFL/B15DT at the higher end.
keyPoints: Rebuilt A14NET petrol: £1,295-£2,450 supply only
Rebuilt A17DTR/B16DTH diesel: £1,450-£2,950 supply only
Second-gen B12XFL/B15DT: £1,650-£3,190 due to PSA complexity
Supply & fit adds £450-£850 labour depending on garage location
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Vauxhall Mokka engine prices for your variant

items.question: What engine codes fit the Vauxhall Mokka?
items.answer: Vauxhall Mokka engine codes include A14NET, A17DTR, B16DTH for first-gen (2012-2019) and B12XFL, B15DT for second-gen (2020+), with exact fitment verified via registration or VIN lookup.
keyPoints: First-gen petrol: A14NET (1.4T), A16XER (1.6 NA), A16LET (1.6T)
First-gen diesel: A17DTJ, A17DTR, A17FEL (1.7L), B16DTH (1.6L)
Second-gen petrol: B12XFL (1.2 PureTech), B15XFT (1.5T)
Second-gen diesel: B15DT (1.5 BlueHDi)
items.cta: Check Vauxhall Mokka engine code compatibility by registration

items.question: Can I upgrade my Vauxhall Mokka engine to a more powerful one?
items.answer: Engine upgrades on Vauxhall Mokka are complex due to ECU, gearbox and drivetrain compatibility; sticking with original-spec rebuilt engines is recommended for reliability and warranty validity.
keyPoints: ECU/immobiliser sync required for any engine code change
Gearbox and drivetrain must match power/torque output
Insurance implications and potential warranty invalidation
Original-spec rebuilt engines offer best value and reliability

Selling as non-runner attracts significant price discount from buyers
